title: rhinanthus serotinus subsp. angustifolius
intro: Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. angustifolius, also known as late flowered yellow rampion, is a plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant that gets some of its nutrients by attaching to the roots of other plants, and it is commonly found in the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. angustifolius include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant has been used in herbal preparations to make teas and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as relieving coughs and colds, as well as supporting the immune system.
